mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when FEDFUNDS is trending down over the prior quarter.
failure_condition: The signal is rejected if 20-day forward returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates above the prior 12-month trend, real-rate uncertainty rises and risk assets reprice lower over the next month, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation rises above its 12-month moving average by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes are followed by non-negative 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average excess return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and tighter credit conditions, which depresses equity demand and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not negative on average after unemployment jumps, or if triggered episodes produce positive returns in more than 40% of cases over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth is a direct demand proxy: sustained gains in PAYEMS imply expanding incomes and consumption, which lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive payroll momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales momentum captures near-term consumer demand; when RSAFS rises faster than its recent trend, revenue expectations improve and S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change is positive and above its 6-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if average 5-day returns after triggers are not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production acceleration indicates improving operating leverage and cyclical earnings strength, so rising INDPRO over the prior quarter should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO do not correspond to positive 20-day forward returns on average, or if the sign is not stable across at least two consecutive 2-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and retail sales together identify a synchronized domestic demand upswing: when HOUST and RSAFS both accelerate, equities should rise over the next 60 trading days through stronger growth expectations and cyclical breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if combined-positive episodes fail to produce positive returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-rate environment, upside surprises in payrolls and industrial production improve growth expectations without immediate policy tightening, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; the effect weakens once rates rise materially.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not outperform single-variable labor or activity signals during zero-rate periods, or if the positive return effect disappears once FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%.
